strengths: 20 years of hands-on, polyglot engineering across high-scale distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby), cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), and full-stack product work — comfortably exceeds the 15-year minimum and demonstrates breadth across the role's technology focus areas.

Actively building AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq right now: MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow frameworks position him as a practitioner in the exact space the role is meant to champion, not just an observer.

Proven entrepreneurial ownership through founding GISCollective (open-source, cloud-deployable geo-data platform, 2016–present) and a consistent record of public knowledge-sharing via conference talks and full-day workshops — directly matching the JD's expectation to inspire others internally and externally.
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; both bachelor's degrees are in adjacent disciplines (CS, Fine Arts/Image Processing), leaving a credential gap against the preferred qualifications.

Current and recent titles are Senior rather than Staff or Principal, suggesting limited prior experience operating at the Director-level IC scope of shaping platform strategy, advising strategic customers, or influencing engineering roadmaps across organizations.

AI platform experience, while genuine and timely, is concentrated in the current role (under 2 years); there are no prior demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale that would establish 'recognized expertise' comparable to candidates with longer AI specialization histories.

key_factors.factor: Seniority and strategic scope gap vs. Director-level IC

response.justification: Bogdan is a self-directed, polyglot engineer with 20 years of shipping across stacks and domains — a pattern that repeatedly shows him entering unfamiliar territory (taking over iOS development solo, building high-throughput D-lang microservices, now authoring MCP code generators for agentic workflows) and delivering. That current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq is the strongest signal: he is already building the category of tooling this role exists to strategize around, which gives him a credibility floor most candidates at this stage of the AI wave lack. His GISCollective founding tenure and public workshop/speaking record map directly onto the JD's expectation for entrepreneurial ownership and inspiring others. The honest stretch is operating scope — he has not yet advised strategic customers or driven platform roadmaps at organizational scale — but his demonstrated velocity across paradigms and the timeliness of his AI specialization make him a candidate whose upside in this direction deserves a closer look.
